content.text: A growing collection of all things Iniquity. Past and present. For use in helping build your bbs.

```typescript
import { Iniquity } from "@iniquitybbs/core"
import { Assets } from "@iniquitybbs/assets"

const iq = new Iniquity()

const welcomeArt = iq.artwork({ basepath: "./iniquity/bbs/assets", filename: Assets.sm_iniq2 })
welcomeArt.render({ clearScreenBefore: true, speed: 100 })

iq.print(`Iniquity comes packed with easy to use assets like ${welcomeArt.filename}`).pause()

iq.hangup()
```

summary.text:
The definitive collection of iniquity ansi/asci/pescii text documents.
Collected from various artpacks over the decades, and various incarnations of iniquity.

content.text: What I hope will be a really cool way of accessing all of your ANSI/ASCII/PETSCII/GIF/JPEG whatever files.
The super cool command line interface to Iniquity.
Invoking via the shell

```shell
iq init --help
```
Invoking via yargs programatically

```typescript
import Init from "@iniquitybbs/cli/dist/src/commands/init"
const init: yargs.CommandModule = new Init()
```
The main entry into all iniquity cli commands that are available.

children.name: describe
children.defaultValue: "Initialize the current directory as an iniquity 3 application."

content.text: Built-in terminal client to connect to an Iniquity BBS.
Invoking via the shell

```shell
iq term
iq term localhost --port 2323
```

children.name: describe

type.value: Connect to an Iniquity BBS with the built-in terminal client.

children.defaultValue: "Connect to an Iniquity BBS with the built-in terminal client."

content.text: The main entry into all iniquity cli commands that are available.
The main entry into all iniquity cli commands that are available.
Launch the Electron desktop app (BBS term in a window).

```ts
iq desktop
```

children.name: describe

type.value: Launch IQ term Desktop (Electron app with BBS term in a window).

children.defaultValue: "Launch IQ term Desktop (Electron app with BBS term in a window)."

content.text: BBS configuration management and system settings

summary.text: IQConfig class for managing BBS configuration
Get a specific config value by path (e.g., 'server.name')
Check if config was loaded from file
Reset a specific section to defaults
Set a specific config value by path (e.g., 'server.name')
When to apply or ask for UTF-8: detect (auto), ask (prompt), cp437/utf8 (force)

content.text: IQ base class and Runtime for BBS applications

summary.text:
Runtime context for BBS execution
Primary coordinator for all runtime components
Ask the user a question and return their input
Clear the last-rendered art key (e.g. after full-screen goodbye so next menu full-redraws).
Get the art key of the last menu that drew artwork (used to skip redraw when same art).
Check if pause was aborted by user
Display pause prompt and wait for key
Print text with automatic pause at page length

Process any queued snacks (draw one, then after duration clear and process next).
Called from menu loop or session tick. No-op if output does not support snacks.
Cursor is restored by showSnack (save/restore) so behavior is correct in any context.
Display raw text without MCI processing
Set the art key after drawing menu artwork (so next menu can skip if same).
Set program directory for asset resolution

Set the current prompt position (1-based). Called by the menu so the cursor
can be restored to the prompt after a snack is cleared.

content.text: ANSI artwork rendering with SAUCE metadata support and CP437 encoding

summary.text: Artwork class for rendering ANSI art files with SAUCE metadata support
Get raw content of artwork file without rendering
Get SAUCE metadata for the last rendered artwork
Render ANSI artwork to the screen
Load artwork into a Graphic object instead of rendering
Chainable functions returned by artwork render

Centering mode for artwork positioning based on screen resolution.
- 'auto': Center if artwork is smaller than screen (default)
- 'horizontal': Center horizontally only
- 'vertical': Center vertically only
- 'both': Always center both axes
- 'none': No centering, render at 1,1 (legacy behavior)
Display style: 'line' renders line-by-line, 'character' renders char-by-char, 'instant' renders all at once

Output encoding for the artwork.
- 'cp437': Send raw CP437 bytes (for BBS terminals like SyncTERM)
- 'utf8': Convert CP437 to UTF-8 (for modern terminals like xterm)
Defaults to 'cp437' for traditional BBS compatibility.

content.text: Use 'display' instead

summary.text: Process MCI/pipe codes in artwork. Defaults to true for BBS compatibility.

Convert a CP437-encoded string to UTF-8
Preserves ANSI escape sequences (they use only ASCII characters)

content.text: UTF-8 encoded string

summary.text: String with CP437 character codes (read as latin1)

content.text:
10x6 cell user avatars with validation and identicon generation

Inspired by Synchronet's avatar_lib.js, this provides:
- Fixed size: 10 columns x 6 rows (120 bytes)
- Store as base64-encoded BIN data
- Draw at absolute screen position
- Validate avatar data (no control characters)
- Auto-generate identicons for users without avatars

summary.text: Avatar class for small user graphics
Create a blank avatar with specified colors
Draw avatar at absolute screen position using Graphic.draw()

Convert a Graphic to avatar data (base64-encoded BIN)
The graphic must be exactly 10x6 or will be cropped/padded
Create avatar from text (simple text rendering)

Generate an identicon avatar from a seed string
Creates a simple symmetric pattern based on the hash
Check if avatar data is enabled and valid

Validate avatar data
Returns true if the data is valid for use as an avatar
Load avatar from a BIN file
Show avatar at current cursor position using console output

content.text:
High-level declarative API for building BBS applications

This module provides a simplified, declarative API that wraps the existing
core functionality, enabling cleaner and more expressive BBS development.

summary.text: Options for artwork rendering via bbs.art()
Data for MCI code interpolation (accessible as @KEY@ in artwork)
Override session encoding for this artwork (default: use session preference)
Pause after rendering (true for default pause, string for custom prompt)
Optional description shown below the label
Menu item definition for declarative menus
Action to perform: "back", "quit", or a function
Name of another menu to navigate to
Explicit X position for this item (bypasses layout system)
Explicit Y position for this item (bypasses layout system)

Centering mode for menu artwork positioning based on screen resolution.
- 'auto': Center if artwork is smaller than screen (default)
- 'horizontal': Center horizontally only
- 'vertical': Center vertically only
- 'both': Always center both axes
- 'none': No centering, render at 1,1 (legacy behavior)
Explicit X position for artwork (1-indexed). Overrides centering.
Explicit Y position for artwork (1-indexed). Overrides centering.
Column 1 (left) X position for two-column layout
Column 2 (right) X position for two-column layout
Enable keyboard hotkeys for menu items (default true)
Starting X position for single-column layout
Starting Y position for items (used by both layouts if col1Y/col2Y not set)
Enable SGR mouse for clicking items (default true)
MCI string for "pressed" look when an item is clicked (e.g. "|15|16")
X position for prompt (if not specified, auto-centered)
Y position for prompt (if not specified, uses layout default)
Message to show when data is empty (for dataPopup)
Duration in ms before the snack fades (default 3000)
Session info for online users display
Target for snack delivery: current session, broadcast, or specific node/user

content.text:
CTerm/SyncTERM feature detection and terminal queries

Inspired by Synchronet's cterm_lib.js, this provides:
- Query terminal capabilities
- Detect CTerm version
- Check for specific feature support (fonts, sixel, palette)

summary.text: CTerm class for terminal capability detection
Build capabilities object from version number
Generate sequence to load a font
Generate sequence to enable/disable mouse button events
Generate sequence to enable/disable mouse motion events
Generate sequence to enable/disable SGR mouse mode
Generate sequence to enable/disable mouse tracking

Parse device attributes response
Returns version number or -1 if not CTerm

Query device attributes (DA) sequence
Response format: ESC[=67;84;101;114;109;MAJOR;MINOR;ATTRIBUTESc

Query font dimensions sequence
Response format: ESC[=3;HEIGHT;WIDTHn

Query font state sequence
Response format: ESC[=1;FIRST;RESULT;STYLE0;STYLE1;STYLE2;STYLE3n

Query graphics dimensions (XTSRGA) sequence
Response format: ESC[?2;0;WIDTH;HEIGHTS

Query enabled modes sequence
Response format: ESC[=2;MODE1;MODE2;...n
Generate sequence to reset palette to defaults
Generate sequence to select a font for a style
Generate sequence to set palette color
Check if a specific feature is supported based on version
Check if palette manipulation is supported
Check if Sixel graphics are supported
CTerm version constants for feature support

content.text: Utility decorators for runtime class enhancement
- Cache method results with optional TTL
- Debounce method calls
- Track open/close state for stateful components
- Performance monitoring for methods
- Automatic retry on failure
- Prevent concurrent execution with queue
- Throttle method calls
- Add timeout to async operations
- Wrap database operations with transaction semantics
- Input validation decorator

summary.text: Validation function returning true or error message

content.text:
Publish/subscribe event bus for BBS-wide event handling

This module provides a global event bus that allows events to be published
and processed regardless of which menu a user is currently viewing.

summary.text:
IQEventBus - Global event bus for BBS applications

Provides a publish/subscribe pattern for decoupled event handling.
Events can be emitted from anywhere and handlers will be called
regardless of the current menu or screen state.
Clear all pending events from the queue
Emit an event and process immediately (synchronous dispatch)
Get the number of pending events
Get list of registered event types
Check if an event has any handlers
Check if there are pending events in the queue
Specific handler to remove (if omitted, removes all handlers for event)

content.text:
Cell-based ANSI manipulation for storing and rendering graphics

Inspired by Synchronet's graphic.js library, this class provides:
- Cell-based data structure (character + attribute per cell)
- Load ANS/BIN files into memory
- Draw to screen at specific coordinates
- Partial region drawing
- Scroll, clear, putmsg operations
- Export to ANSI string or BIN format

True height of content (may be less than allocated height)
Convert CGA attribute to ANSI escape sequence
Create a copy of this graphic
Draw the graphic to output at specified position
Load a file into the graphic
Put a message at position with optional attribute

If true, process all MCI codes (pipe codes, @-codes, control codes) before parsing ANSI
A single cell in the graphic buffer

content.text:
Display sixel graphics in compatible terminals

Inspired by Synchronet's showsixel.js, this provides:
- Detect Sixel support via CTerm version check
- Display pre-rendered Sixel files
- Basic sixel sequence generation

Sixel is a bitmap graphics format for terminals, supported by:
- SyncTERM (CTerm 1.189+)
- xterm (with sixel support compiled in)
- mlterm
- mintty
- foot

summary.text: Minimum CTerm version for Sixel support
Generate carriage return (move to start of next sixel row)
Display sixel data from a buffer

Encode a sixel character
A sixel character represents 6 vertical pixels

Generate a simple test pattern
Useful for testing sixel support
Check if data appears to be sixel format
Check if Sixel is supported based on CTerm version
Generate line feed (move down one sixel row)

Parse sixel image info from data
Returns basic info about the sixel image
Strip sixel data from a string (for text extraction)

BBS singleton object - the main interface for building BBS applications
Provides declarative menu registration, artwork rendering, user management,
event bus, and more.

content.text:
```typescript
import { bbs, screen } from "@iniquitybbs/core"

screen.setResolution(132, 37)
bbs.menu("main", { ... })
bbs.start(async () => {
    await bbs.showMenu("main")
})
```

content.text: Opt-in string manipulation utilities (no global pollution)
Text with newlines prepended
Centered text with padding

```typescript
import { centerText } from '@iniquitybbs/core/utils'
const centered = centerText("Hello", 80)
```
Colorized text with ANSI codes

```typescript
import { colorText } from '@iniquitybbs/core/utils'
const colored = colorText("Hello", "cyan")
```

summary.text: Position cursor at coordinates before displaying text

content.text: Text with cursor positioning
Left-aligned text with right padding
Lowercase text
Padded text

```typescript
import { padText } from '@iniquitybbs/core/utils'
const padded = padText("Hello", 10, ' ', 'right')  // "Hello     "
```

summary.text: Character to use for padding (defaults to space)

content.text: Repeated text
Right-aligned text with left padding

summary.text: Strip ANSI escape codes from text

content.text: Text with ANSI codes removed

```typescript
import { stripAnsi } from '@iniquitybbs/core/utils'
const plain = stripAnsi("\x1b[1;37mHello\x1b[0m")  // "Hello"
```
Title-cased text
Truncated text

summary.text: Suffix to add if truncated (defaults to '...')

content.text: Uppercase text

summary.text: Get visible length of text (excluding ANSI codes)

content.text: Visible character count

```typescript
import { visibleLength } from '@iniquitybbs/core/utils'
const len = visibleLength("\x1b[1;37mHello\x1b[0m")  // 5
```

Read and display XBin image format files

Inspired by Synchronet's xbin_lib.js and xbimage_lib.js, this provides:
- Read XBin files (custom font + character data)
- Parse XBin header (width, height, font data, flags)
- Display XBin images (requires font loading)

XBin is an extended BIN format that can include:
- Custom palettes
- Custom fonts
- Compressed data

summary.text: XBin class for reading and displaying XBin images
Clean up after displaying XBin (reset fonts and palette)
Create an XBin file from a Graphic
Get info about an XBin file without fully parsing it
Check if a file is an XBin file
Check if buffer contains XBin data
Parse XBin data from a buffer
XBin palette (48 bytes - 16 colors x 3 RGB values)

IQ term Desktop — Electron main process.
Opens a window to the BBS term page; optionally starts the server if not running.

Default permissions for different access levels
Initialize the group database with a BBS-specific data path

content.text: Server-side components for running BBS applications

summary.text:
Minimal HTTP API server wrapping Ollama for BBS AI chat.
Serves POST /api/v1/ai and GET /api/v1/health.
BBS root directory (containing iniquity.json and optionally package.json). Used to build AI system context.

Create and start an HTTP server that proxies chat requests to Ollama.
Returns the server instance so the caller can close it.

content.text: Execute BBS programs written in TypeScript
Per-connection session state and management implementing IQOutput

summary.text: Session class implements IQOutput interface for use with core Runtime
Node number assigned by the server
Username when logged in (set by BBS layer for snack target by user)
Sentinel for mouse events in the queue (followed by "x:y")
Whether the client suggested UTF-8 (iq term handshake or TERM type)
Get terminal encoding preference (set at connect)
Get pending actions from MCI processing
Check if input is available without blocking
Check if there are pending actions
Process MCI codes in text without writing
Read a single key press (returns the character)

content.text: The key pressed, or null if no input available

summary.text: Read a line of input from the user
Set terminal encoding; updates MCI context for @CHARSET@ etc.

Set the callback run every 10ms while waitKey() is active (e.g. processQueue + processSnacks).
Used so snacks and events are processed when waiting in popup/pause/choice etc.
Check if client supports font switching
Check if client supports iCE colors

Wait for any key press (does not echo).
While waiting, if a tick callback is set, runs it every 10ms (serialized, no overlap).
Write data to socket (converts to UTF-8 when session encoding is utf8)
Write with MCI processing enabled (respects session encoding)
True if client sent IQTERM UTF-8 handshake or TERM type suggests UTF-8
Input mode determines how incoming characters are handled

content.text: Node.js based Telnet server for BBS connections

summary.text: Get server info in BBS API format
Get the number of active connections

Handle a new WebSocket connection (same BBS session as TCP, different transport).
Call this from the HTTP server's WebSocket upgrade handler so web/desktop clients get a session.
Get the currently active TelnetServer instance

content.text: Attach WebSocket endpoint to HTTP server and run BBS sessions over WS

summary.text:
Attach a WebSocket server to the existing HTTP server.
When a client connects to path /ws, a BBS session is created via the TelnetServer.
Call after TelnetServer.start() so snack:push and server info are already registered.

content.text: Presents a Node net.Socket-like interface to Session over a WebSocket

summary.text:
Socket-like adapter for WebSocket so Session can use it without change.
Emits "data" (Buffer), "close", "error"; provides write() and end().
Set so Session can skip telnet negotiation (browser doesn't speak telnet).
Create a Socket-like object from a WebSocket for use with Session.

Check if a host is reachable

content.text: Utilities for generating ANSI escape sequences for terminal control

summary.text:
Font alias mapping for SAUCE compatibility
Maps common SAUCE font names to SyncTERM font names

SyncTERM/CTerm Font Table
Maps font names to their ESC sequence codes
See: https://github.com/protomouse/synchronet/blob/master/src/conio/cterm.txt
Create a CGA attribute from foreground, background, and flags
Convert CGA attribute to ANSI escape sequence
Clear the entire screen and move cursor to home position

iCE Colors - Blink to Bright Intensity
Converts blink attribute (bit 7) to bright background colors
Essential for most modern BBS ANSI art
Get SyncTERM font name from alias (e.g., SAUCE font name)
Set SyncTERM font using alias (handles SAUCE font names)
CGA Color attribute constants for cell-based graphics

content.text: Decorators for class-based BBS modules

decorator
Marks a class as an Iniquity module and stores configuration

decorator
Marks a method as the runtime entry point for a module

summary.text: Get module metadata for a constructor
Get runtime metadata for a method

content.text: Comprehensive MCI/macro code system combining Synchronet, Iniquity, ENiGMA, and Oblivion/2
Synchronet-compatible @-codes for dynamic content expansion
Screen and flow control codes (|CS, |PA, |DE, etc.)
Oblivion/2 and Synchronet compatible pipe color codes
Original Iniquity-style position markers (^X01-^X99)

Context interfaces and providers for MCI code processing

Provides context data for @-code expansion including user, system, BBS,
terminal, message, and file contexts. Compatible with Synchronet,
original Iniquity, and ENiGMA BBS systems.

summary.text:
BBS context for MCI code expansion
Contains BBS configuration and identity

File context for MCI code expansion
Contains current file/area information
Complete MCI context combining all context types
Context provider interface for dynamic context updates

Message context for MCI code expansion
Contains current message/area information
Node context for multi-node BBS systems

System context for MCI code expansion
Contains system-level information

Terminal context for MCI code expansion
Contains terminal capabilities and settings

User context for MCI code expansion
Contains all user-related data that can be referenced via @-codes
Create a complete default MCI context
Default system context with current date/time
Default user context with sensible defaults
Merge partial context into existing context
